steps.name: Availability check with 30s cache + ALLOW_PAID enforcement (BR-1)
Failover logic — QuotaError/Timeout/5xx vs. non-retryable 4xx (BR-2, BR-4)
Circuit breaker — 60s exclusion after health-check fail (BR-5)
Wire up tests + verify all Acceptance Criteria

decisions: 2026-07-17 reconciliation: merged to main (backend/app/core/router.py, 413 lines, chain/failover logic present). Marking done.

2026-07-17 (later): full pytest run revealed ProviderRouter.__init__() didn't accept the settings= kwarg tests/unit/core/test_router.py expects, plus router.py referenced undefined module-level helpers _cache_key/_now_iso (test file also used _cache_key without importing it). Fixed via feat/3-2-router-settings-fix (branch name deviates from feat/3-2-chain-router-failover-allow-paid because that branch was a stale pre-merge snapshot, ~166 files diverged from main -- reused a differently-named branch rather than resetting/deleting a branch this session didn't create). Also found and fixed a real AC5 gap: router.call() never invoked adapter.available() as a health pre-flight, so HealthBoomAdapter's simulated health-check crash never reached the circuit breaker; added a pre-flight check before the method-invocation try block that trips the circuit and re-raises on an unexpected exception (kept out of the generic retry catch-all per rules/error-handling.md). All 9 tests/unit/core/test_router.py cases pass; fast-forward-merged into origin/main at 5f143d6.
